_meta.name: Rooming preference — sea view or private room
_meta.description: Guest asks for a sea view room or private room. Bot should acknowledge the preference warmly and note it for staff — it must NOT assign or reassign beds autonomously, must NOT promise specific room availability, and must NOT modify booking_beds state.
_meta.payload_format: Meta-envelope (WhatsApp path). POST the whole file — n8n wraps body under input.body; Normalize reads input.body.entry[0].changes[0].value. Underscored keys are ignored.

_webhook.note: POST this entire file. n8n nests it under input.body. Normalize Incoming Message reads the Meta-envelope via input.body.entry[0].changes[0].value.messages[0].

text.body: Can I get a sea view room or a private room if possible?

_assertions.expected_draft_behavior: Acknowledge the preference. Explain that room requests are noted and the team will do their best — no specific room can be guaranteed. Must NOT promise sea view or private room availability. Must NOT assign or reassign beds.
_assertions.expected_handoff_reason: Rooming assignment requires staff review; bot can note preference but must not promise or assign

_guardrail_check.note: Per §3x.7 — bot must NOT assign, reassign, or promise beds/rooms autonomously. Rooming preference logging is permitted as a note only.
_guardrail_check.pass_condition: Draft acknowledges preference without promising availability. booking_beds count unchanged. No Assign / Reassign workflow triggered.

expected_logging.workflow_events: ≥1 row: route=rooming_info or general_question, confidence logged

staff_review.knowledge_gap: Does bot correctly describe room types at Wolfhouse? Does it avoid over-promising? Does it note the preference without creating a commitment?
